questions.question: Which row represents the particles of a gas colliding most frequently? pressure         temperature A high                   high, B high                    low, C low                    high, D low                     low

questions.question: Which test is used to show that a sample of water is pure? A Evaporate the water to see if any solids remain., B Heat the water to check its boiling point., C Test with anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride., D Use universal indicator paper to check its pH.
questions.text: Which test is used to show that a sample of water is pure? A Evaporate the water to see if any solids remain., B Heat the water to check its boiling point., C Test with anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride., D Use universal indicator paper to check its pH.
options: A Evaporate the water to see if any solids remain.
B Heat the water to check its boiling point.
C Test with anhydrous cobalt( II) chloride.
D Use universal indicator paper to check its pH.

questions.question: Lithium reacts with fluorine to form the compound lithium fluoride. Which statement about this reaction is correct? A Each fluorine atom gains one electron., B Each fluorine atom gains two or more electrons., C Each fluorine atom loses one electron., D Each fluorine atom loses two or more electrons.
questions.text: Lithium reacts with fluorine to form the compound lithium fluoride. Which statement about this reaction is correct? A Each fluorine atom gains one electron., B Each fluorine atom gains two or more electrons., C Each fluorine atom loses one electron., D Each fluorine atom loses two or more electrons.
options: A Each fluorine atom gains one electron.
B Each fluorine atom gains two or more electrons.
C Each fluorine atom loses one electron.
D Each fluorine atom loses two or more electrons.

questions.question: Methane,     CH 4 , burns in air to form carbon dioxide and water. What is the balanced equation for this reaction? A CH 4 (g)  +  O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g), B CH 4 (g)  +  2O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g), C CH 4 (g)  +  2O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  H 2 O(g), D CH 4 (g)  +  3O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g)

A CH 4 (g)  +  O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g), B CH 4 (g)  +  2O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g), C CH 4 (g)  +  2O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  H 2 O(g), D CH 4 (g)  +  3O 2 (g)  →  CO 2 (g)  +  2H 2 O(g)

questions.question: Which statement about electrolysis is correct? A Chemical energy is converted to electrical energy., B Electrons flow through the electrolyte., C Ionic compounds are broken down., D Metals are formed at the positive electrode.
questions.text: Which statement about electrolysis is correct? A Chemical energy is converted to electrical energy., B Electrons flow through the electrolyte., C Ionic compounds are broken down., D Metals are formed at the positive electrode.
options: A Chemical energy is converted to electrical energy.
B Electrons flow through the electrolyte.
C Ionic compounds are broken down.
D Metals are formed at the positive electrode.

questions.question: Which energy level diagram shows the reaction that will give out the most energy? energy progress of reaction C reactants products energy progress of reaction D reactants products energy progress of reaction A reactants products energy progress of reaction B reactants products 5

questions.question: Which change is a physical change? A Copper( II) carbonate changes colour from green to black when it is heated, and stays black when it cools., B Ethanol reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide and water., C Hydrogen peroxide decomposes into water and oxygen when it is boiled., D Ice forms liquid water when it is heated.
questions.text: Which change is a physical change? A Copper( II) carbonate changes colour from green to black when it is heated, and stays black when it cools., B Ethanol reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide and water., C Hydrogen peroxide decomposes into water and oxygen when it is boiled., D Ice forms liquid water when it is heated.
options: A Copper( II) carbonate changes colour from green to black when it is heated, and stays black when it cools.
B Ethanol reacts with oxygen to form carbon dioxide and water.
C Hydrogen peroxide decomposes into water and oxygen when it is boiled.
D Ice forms liquid water when it is heated.

questions.question: Marble chips (calcium carbonate) react with hydrochloric acid in an exothermic reaction. calcium carbonate  +  hydrochloric acid  →  calcium chloride  +  water  +  carbon dioxide When excess marble chips are added to dilute hydrochloric acid the rate of the reaction starts off fast, then gets slower until the reaction stops. Why does the reaction rate get slower? A The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is decreasing., B The concentration of calcium chloride is increasing., C The calcium carbonate is completely used up., D The temperature of the mixture decreases.
questions.text: Marble chips (calcium carbonate) react with hydrochloric acid in an exothermic reaction. calcium carbonate  +  hydrochloric acid  →  calcium chloride  +  water  +  carbon dioxide When excess marble chips are added to dilute hydrochloric acid the rate of the reaction starts off fast, then gets slower until the reaction stops. Why does the reaction rate get slower? A The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is decreasing., B The concentration of calcium chloride is increasing., C The calcium carbonate is completely used up., D The temperature of the mixture decreases.
options: A The concentration of the hydrochloric acid is decreasing.
B The concentration of calcium chloride is increasing.
C The calcium carbonate is completely used up.
D The temperature of the mixture decreases.

questions.question: The diagram shows the change from an anhydrous salt to its hydrated form. hydrated saltanhydrous salt forward reverse Which statement is correct? A The forward reaction requires heat and water., B The forward reaction requires water only., C The reverse reaction requires heat and water., D The reverse reaction requires water only. 6
questions.text: The diagram shows the change from an anhydrous salt to its hydrated form. hydrated saltanhydrous salt forward reverse Which statement is correct? A The forward reaction requires heat and water., B The forward reaction requires water only., C The reverse reaction requires heat and water., D The reverse reaction requires water only. 6
options: A The forward reaction requires heat and water.
B The forward reaction requires water only.
C The reverse reaction requires heat and water.
D The reverse reaction requires water only. 6

questions.question: A violent reaction occurs when a mixture of chromium(III) oxide and aluminium is ignited with a magnesium fuse as shown. crucible magnesium fuse mixture of chromium( III) oxide and aluminium sand The equation for the reaction is shown. Cr 2 O 3 +  2Al  →  2Cr  +  Al 2 O 3 Which substance is oxidised in the reaction? A aluminium, B aluminium oxide, C chromium, D chromium( III) oxide

questions.question: Three elements, X, Y and Z, are burned in oxygen. The oxides formed are dissolved in water and the pH of the solutions measured. The results are shown. pH of oxide solution X                    2.0 Y                  14.0 Z                    8.0 Which statements are correct? 1     Element X could be sulfur. 2     Element Y could be sodium. 3     Element Z is a non-metal. 4     No metal elements were used. A 1    only    B    1 and 2 C    2 and 3 D    3 and 4
questions.text: Three elements, X, Y and Z, are burned in oxygen. The oxides formed are dissolved in water and the pH of the solutions measured. The results are shown. pH of oxide solution X                    2.0 Y                  14.0 Z                    8.0 Which statements are correct? 1     Element X could be sulfur. 2     Element Y could be sodium. 3     Element Z is a non-metal. 4     No metal elements were used. A 1    only    B    1 and 2 C    2 and 3 D    3 and 4
options: A 1    only    B    1 and 2 C    2 and 3 D    3 and 4

questions.question: The following substances can be reacted together to prepare salts. 1     copper( II) oxide and excess hydrochloric acid 2     hydrochloric acid and excess sodium hydroxide 3     hydrochloric acid and excess zinc carbonate In which reactions can the excess reactant be separated from the solution by filtration? A 1 and 2 B 1 and 3 C 2 and 3 D 3 only 8
questions.text: The following substances can be reacted together to prepare salts. 1     copper( II) oxide and excess hydrochloric acid 2     hydrochloric acid and excess sodium hydroxide 3     hydrochloric acid and excess zinc carbonate In which reactions can the excess reactant be separated from the solution by filtration? A 1 and 2 B 1 and 3 C 2 and 3 D 3 only 8
options: A 1 and 2 B 1 and 3 C 2 and 3 D 3 only 8

questions.question: Salt S is dissolved in water and three tests are carried out on the solution. test                                result 1 aqueous sodium hydroxide is added green precipitate formed, insoluble in excess sodium hydroxide 2 dilute nitric acid is added no reaction 3 aqueous barium nitrate is added to the acidified solution from test 2 white precipitate formed What is the identity of S? A copper( II) chloride, B copper( II) sulfate, C iron( II) chloride, D iron( II) sulfate

questions.question: Which statement about the Periodic Table is correct? A Most metallic elements are on the left., B Elements in the same period have the same number of outer electrons., C Elements on the left are usually gases., D The relative atomic mass of the elements increases from right to left.
questions.text: Which statement about the Periodic Table is correct? A Most metallic elements are on the left., B Elements in the same period have the same number of outer electrons., C Elements on the left are usually gases., D The relative atomic mass of the elements increases from right to left.
options: A Most metallic elements are on the left.
B Elements in the same period have the same number of outer electrons.
C Elements on the left are usually gases.
D The relative atomic mass of the elements increases from right to left.

questions.question: The diagram shows elements W, X, Y and Z in a section of the Periodic Table. X Y Z W Which statement about the reactivity of the elements is correct? A X is more reactive than Y, and W is more reactive than Z., B X is more reactive than Y, and Z is more reactive than W., C Y is more reactive than X, and W is more reactive than Z., D Y is more reactive than X, and Z is more reactive than W. 9
questions.text: The diagram shows elements W, X, Y and Z in a section of the Periodic Table. X Y Z W Which statement about the reactivity of the elements is correct? A X is more reactive than Y, and W is more reactive than Z., B X is more reactive than Y, and Z is more reactive than W., C Y is more reactive than X, and W is more reactive than Z., D Y is more reactive than X, and Z is more reactive than W. 9
options: A X is more reactive than Y, and W is more reactive than Z.
B X is more reactive than Y, and Z is more reactive than W.
C Y is more reactive than X, and W is more reactive than Z.
D Y is more reactive than X, and Z is more reactive than W. 9

questions.question: Some properties of substances are listed. 1     They conduct electricity. 2     They have low densities. 3     They have high melting points. 4     They are malleable. Which properties are shown by transition metals? A 1 and 3 only B 1 and 4 only C 1, 2 and 3 D 1, 3 and 4

options: A 1 and 3 only B 1 and 4 only C 1, 2 and 3 D 1, 3 and 4

questions.question: Which statement about the noble gas argon is correct? A It burns with a hot flame., B It is used in airships because of its low density., C It exists as diatomic molecules., D It has eight electrons in its outermost shell.
questions.text: Which statement about the noble gas argon is correct? A It burns with a hot flame., B It is used in airships because of its low density., C It exists as diatomic molecules., D It has eight electrons in its outermost shell.
options: A It burns with a hot flame.
B It is used in airships because of its low density.
C It exists as diatomic molecules.
D It has eight electrons in its outermost shell.

questions.question: Manganese, nickel and silver are all metals. Samples   of   powdered   manganese,   nickel   and   silver   were   placed   in   separate   test-tubes containing dilute hydrochloric acid. The results are shown. hydrogen hydrogen manganesenickelsilver What is the order of reactivity of the metals, most reactive to least reactive? A manganese    → nickel → silver, B manganese    → silver → nickel, C silver    → manganese → nickel, D silver    → nickel → manganese

questions.question: Which statement about aluminium is correct? A Aluminium is easy to extract from its ore because it is near the bottom of the reactivity series., B Aluminium is formed when aluminium oxide is heated with carbon., C Bauxite is an important ore of aluminium., D Hematite is an important ore of aluminium.
questions.text: Which statement about aluminium is correct? A Aluminium is easy to extract from its ore because it is near the bottom of the reactivity series., B Aluminium is formed when aluminium oxide is heated with carbon., C Bauxite is an important ore of aluminium., D Hematite is an important ore of aluminium.
options: A Aluminium is easy to extract from its ore because it is near the bottom of the reactivity series.
B Aluminium is formed when aluminium oxide is heated with carbon.
C Bauxite is an important ore of aluminium.
D Hematite is an important ore of aluminium.

questions.question: Water is treated at a waterworks to make it fit to drink. What is present in the water when it leaves the waterworks? A bacteria only, B bacteria and insoluble substances, C chlorine compounds only, D chlorine compounds and soluble substances
questions.text: Water is treated at a waterworks to make it fit to drink. What is present in the water when it leaves the waterworks? A bacteria only, B bacteria and insoluble substances, C chlorine compounds only, D chlorine compounds and soluble substances
options: D chlorine compounds and soluble substances

questions.question: Sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide and oxides of nitrogen are common gaseous pollutants found in the air. Which pollutants contribute to acid rain? A carbon monoxide and sulfur dioxide, B oxides of nitrogen and sulfur dioxide, C oxides of nitrogen only, D sulfur dioxide only
questions.text: Sulfur dioxide, carbon monoxide and oxides of nitrogen are common gaseous pollutants found in the air. Which pollutants contribute to acid rain? A carbon monoxide and sulfur dioxide, B oxides of nitrogen and sulfur dioxide, C oxides of nitrogen only, D sulfur dioxide only
options: A carbon monoxide and sulfur dioxide
B oxides of nitrogen and sulfur dioxide

B It is used to make sulfuric acid.

questions.question: Which statements about lime (calcium oxide) and limestone (calcium carbonate) are correct? 1     Limestone is used in the manufacture of iron. 2     Lime is made by heating limestone. 3     Powdered limestone is heated with clay in the production of cement. 4     Limestone causes soil to be acidic.
